title: Algal-bloom early warning for coastal aquaculture
summary: CMEMS biogeochemistry and Sentinel-3 ocean-colour data gave early warning of harmful algal blooms threatening fish farms.
narrative:
## Challenge
Harmful algal blooms threatened aquaculture and bathing waters, but sparse ground sampling meant blooms were often detected only after damage occurred.

## Approach
CMEMS biogeochemistry products and Sentinel-3 OLCI ocean-colour data tracked chlorophyll and turbidity, providing early warning of bloom development for protective action.

## Results
Producers gained lead time to take protective measures for fish farms and water intakes, reducing losses from bloom events.
